question: In the context of federated learning (FL) using simulated IoT clients, what does a sequence of three transactions sequentially represent?

options.A: A sequence of three transactions represents three independent model updates from similar IoT clients.
options.B: A sequence of three transactions represents the cumulative updates of a single model by three different IoT clients.
options.C: A sequence of three transactions represents three model updates that are dependent on each other sequentially from the same IoT client.
options.D: A sequence of three transactions represents three unrelated updates from different types of IoT devices.

explanation: In the context provided, each transaction represents a model update from a simulated IoT client in an FL round. A sequence of three transactions would thus represent three model updates that are dependent on each other sequentially from the same IoT client, building upon each update.
